25→\n    26→/**\n    27→ * Parse agent JSON output to extract token usage from step_finish events\n    28→ * Agent outputs NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON) with step_finish events containing token data\n    29→ * @param {string} output - Raw stdout output from agent command\n    30→ * @returns {Object} Aggregated token usage and cost data\n    31→ */\n    32→export const parseAgentTokenUsage = output => {\n    33→  const usage = {\n    34→    inputTokens: 0,\n    35→    outputTokens: 0,\n    36→    reasoningTokens: 0,\n    37→    cacheReadTokens: 0,\n    38→    cacheWriteTokens: 0,\n    39→    totalCost: 0,\n    40→    stepCount: 0,\n    41→  };\n    42→\n    43→  // Try to parse each line as JSON (agent outputs NDJSON format)\n    44→  const lines = output.split('\\n');\n    45→  for (const line of lines) {\n    46→    const trimmedLine = line.trim();\n    47→    if (!trimmedLine || !trimmedLine.startsWith('{')) continue;\n    48→\n    49→    try {\n    50→      const parsed = JSON.parse(trimmedLine);\n    51→\n    52→      // Look for step_finish events which contain token usage\n    53→      if (parsed.type === 'step_finish' && parsed.part?.tokens) {\n    54→        const tokens = parsed.part.tokens;\n    55→        usage.stepCount++;\n    56→\n    57→        // Add token counts\n    58→        if (tokens.input) usage.inputTokens += tokens.input;\n    59→        if (tokens.output) usage.outputTokens += tokens.output;\n    60→        if (tokens.reasoning) usage.reasoningTokens += tokens.reasoning;\n    61→\n    62→        // Handle cache tokens (can be in different formats)\n    63→        if (tokens.cache) {\n    64→          if (tokens.cache.read) usage.cacheReadTokens += tokens.cache.read;\n    65→          if (tokens.cache.write) usage.cacheWriteTokens += tokens.cache.write;\n    66→        }\n    67→\n    68→        // Add cost from step_finish (usually 0 for free models like grok-code)\n    69→        if (parsed.part.cost !== undefined) {\n    70→          usage.totalCost += parsed.part.cost;\n    71→        }\n    72→      }\n    73→    } catch {\n    74→      // Skip lines that aren't valid JSON\n    75→      continue;\n    76→    }\n    77→  }\n    78→\n    79→  return usage;\n    80→};\n    81→\n    82→/**\n    83→ * Helper function to get original provider name from provider identifier\n    84→ * Used for calculating public pricing estimates based on original provider prices\n    85→ * @param {string} providerId - Provider identifier (e.g., 'openai', 'anthropic', 'moonshot')\n    86→ * @returns {string} Human-readable provider name for pricing reference\n    87→ */\n    88→const getOriginalProviderName = providerId => {\n    89→  if (!providerId) return null;\n    90→\n    91→  const providerMap = {\n    92→    openai: 'OpenAI',\n    93→    anthropic: 'Anthropic',\n    94→    moonshot: 'Moonshot AI',\n    95→    google: 'Google',\n    96→    opencode: 'OpenCode Zen',\n    97→    grok: 'xAI',\n    98→  };\n    99→\n   100→  return providerMap[providerId] || providerId.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + providerId.slice(1);\n   101→};\n   102→\n   103→/**\n   104→ * Calculate pricing for agent tool usage using models.dev API\n   105→ * Issue #1250: Shows actual provider (OpenCode Zen) and calculates public pricing estimate\n   106→ * based on original provider prices (Moonshot AI, OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)\n   107→ *\n   108→ * @param {string} modelId - The model ID used (e.g., 'opencode/grok-code')\n   109→ * @param {Object} tokenUsage - Token usage data from parseAgentTokenUsage\n   110→ * @returns {Object} Pricing information with:\n   111→ *   - provider: Always \"OpenCode Zen\" (actual provider)\n   112→ *   - originalProvider: The original model provider for pricing reference\n   113→ *   - totalCostUSD: Public pricing estimate based on original provider prices\n   114→ *   - opencodeCost: Actual billed cost from OpenCode Zen (free for most models)\n   115→ */\n   116→export const calculateAgentPricing = async (modelId, tokenUsage) => {\n   117→  // Extract the model name from provider/model format\n   118→  // e.g., 'opencode/grok-code' -> 'grok-code'\n   119→  const modelName = modelId.includes('/') ? modelId.split('/').pop() : modelId;\n   120→\n   121→  // Extract provider from model ID to determine original provider for pricing\n   122→  const providerFromModel = modelId.includes('/') ? modelId.split('/')[0] : null;\n   123→\n   124→  // Get original provider name for pricing reference\n   125→  const originalProvider = getOriginalProviderName(providerFromModel);\n   126→\n   127→  try {\n   128→    // Fetch model info from models.dev API\n   129→    const modelInfo = await fetchModelInfo(modelName);\n   130→\n   131→    if (modelInfo && modelInfo.cost) {\n   132→      const cost = modelInfo.cost;\n   133→\n   134→      // Calculate public pricing estimate based on original provider prices\n   135→      // Prices are per 1M tokens, so divide by 1,000,000\n   136→      const inputCost = (tokenUsage.inputTokens * (cost.input || 0)) / 1_000_000;\n   137→      const outputCost = (tokenUsage.outputTokens * (cost.output || 0)) / 1_000_000;\n   138→      const cacheReadCost = (tokenUsage.cacheReadTokens * (cost.cache_read || 0)) / 1_000_000;\n   139→      const cacheWriteCost = (tokenUsage.cacheWriteTokens * (cost.cache_write || 0)) / 1_000_000;\n   140→\n   141→      const totalCost = inputCost + outputCost + cacheReadCost + cacheWriteCost;\n   142→\n   143→      // Determine if this is a free model from OpenCode Zen\n   144→      // Models accessed via OpenCode Zen are free, regardless of original provider pricing\n   145→      const isOpencodeFreeModel = providerFromModel === 'opencode' || modelName.toLowerCase().includes('free') || modelName.toLowerCase().includes('grok') || providerFromModel === 'moonshot' || providerFromModel === 'openai' || providerFromModel === 'anthropic';\n   146→\n   147→      return {\n   148→        modelId,\n   149→        modelName: modelInfo.name || modelName,\n   150→        // Issue #1250: Always show OpenCode Zen as actual provider\n   151→        provider: 'OpenCode Zen',\n   152→        // Store original provider for reference in pricing display\n   153→        originalProvider: originalProvider || modelInfo.provider || null,\n   154→        pricing: {\n   155→          inputPerMillion: cost.input || 0,\n   156→          outputPerMillion: cost.output || 0,\n   157→          cacheReadPerMillion: cost.cache_read || 0,\n   158→          cacheWritePerMillion: cost.cache_write || 0,\n   159→        },\n   160→        tokenUsage,\n   161→        breakdown: {\n   162→          input: inputCost,\n   163→          output: outputCost,\n   164→          cacheRead: cacheReadCost,\n   165→          cacheWrite: cacheWriteCost,\n   166→        },\n   167→        // Public pricing estimate based on original provider prices\n   168→        totalCostUSD: totalCost,\n   169→        // Actual cost from OpenCode Zen (free for supported models)\n   170→        opencodeCost: isOpencodeFreeModel ? 0 : totalCost,\n   171→        // Keep for backward compatibility - indicates if model has zero pricing\n   172→        isFreeModel: cost.input === 0 && cost.output === 0,\n   173→        // New flag to indicate if OpenCode Zen provides this model for free\n   174→        isOpencodeFreeModel,\n   175→      };\n   176→    }\n   177→    // Model not found in API, return what we have\n   178→    return {\n   179→      modelId,\n   180→      modelName,\n   181→      provider: 'OpenCode Zen',\n   182→      originalProvider,\n   183→      tokenUsage,\n   184→      totalCostUSD: null,\n   185→      opencodeCost: 0, // OpenCode Zen is free\n   186→      isOpencodeFreeModel: true,\n   187→      error: 'Model not found in models.dev API',\n   188→    };\n   189→  } catch (error) {\n   190→    // Error fetching pricing, return with error info\n   191→    return {\n   192→      modelId,\n   193→      modelName,\n   194→      provider: 'OpenCode Zen',\n   195→      originalProvider,\n   196→      tokenUsage,\n   197→      totalCostUSD: null,\n   198→      opencodeCost: 0, // OpenCode Zen is free\n   199→      isOpencodeFreeModel: true,\n   200→      error: error.message,\n   201→    };\n   202→  }\n   203→};\n   204→\n   205→// Model mapping to translate aliases to full model IDs for Agent\n   206→// Agent uses OpenCode Zen's JSON interface and models\n   207→// Issue #1185: Free models  212→    'grok-code-fast-1': 'opencode/grok-code',\n   213→    'big-pickle': 'opencode/big-pickle',\n   214→    'gpt-5-nano': 'opencode/gpt-5-nano',\n   215→    sonnet: 'claude-3.5-haiku': 'anthropic/claude-3.5-haiku',\n   224→    'claude-3.5-sonnet': 'anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet',\n   225→  };\n   226→\n   227→  // Return mapped model ID if it's an alias, otherwise return as-is\n   228→  return modelMap[model] || model;\n   229→};\n   230→\n   231→// Function to validate Agent connection\n   232→export const validateAgentConnection = async (model = 'grok-code-fast-1') => {\n   233→  // Map model alias to full ID\n   234→  const mappedModel = mapModelToId(model);\n   235→\n   236→  // Retry configuration\n   237→  const maxRetries = 3;\n   238→  let retryCount = 0;\n   239→\n   240→  const attemptValidation = async () => {\n   241→    try {\n   242→      if (retryCount === 0) {\n   243→        await log('🔍 Validating Agent connection...');\n   244→      } else {\n   245→        await log(`🔄 Retry attempt ${retryCount}/${maxRetries} for Agent validation...`);\n   246→      }\n   247→\n   248→      // Check if Agent CLI is installed and get version\n   249→      try {\n   250→        const versionResult = await $`timeout ${Math.floor(timeouts.opencodeCli / 1000)} agent --version`;\n   251→        if (versionResult.code === 0) {\n   252→          const version = versionResult.stdout?.toString().trim();\n   253→          if (retryCount === 0) {\n   254→            await log(`📦 Agent CLI version: ${version}`);\n   255→          }\n   256→        }\n   257→      } catch (versionError) {\n   258→        if (retryCount === 0) {\n   259→          await log(`⚠️  Agent CLI version check failed (${versionError.code}), proceeding with connection test...`);\n   260→        }\n   261→      }\n   262→\n   263→      // Test basic Agent functionality with a simple \"hi\" message\n   264→      // Agent uses the same JSON interface as OpenCode\n   265→      const testResult = await $`printf \"hi\" | timeout ${Math.floor(timeouts.opencodeCli / 1000)} agent --model ${mappedModel}`;\n   266→\n   267→      if (testResult.code !== 0) {\n   268→        const stderr = testResult.stderr?.toString() || '';\n   269→\n   270→        if (stderr.includes('auth') || stderr.includes('login')) {\n   271→          await log('❌ Agent authentication failed', { level: 'error' });\n   272→          await log('   💡 Note: Agent uses OpenCode models. Agent validation failed with exit code ${testResult.code}`, { level: 'error' });\n   279→        if (stderr) await log(`   Error: ${stderr.trim()}`, { level: 'error' });\n   280→        return false;\n   281→      }\n   282→\n   283→      // Success\n   284→      await log('✅ Agent connection validated successfully');\n   285→      return true;\n   286→    } catch (error) {\n   287→      await log(`❌ Failed to validate Agent connection: ${error.message}`, { level: 'error' });\n   288→      await log('   💡 Make sure @link-assistant/agent is installed globally: bun install -g attemptValidation();\n   297→};\n   298→\n   299→// Function to handle Agent runtime switching (if applicable)\n   300→export const handleAgentRuntimeSwitch = async () => {\n   301→  // Agent is run not required for this operation');\n   304→};\n   305→\n   306→// Main function to execute Agent with prompts and settings\n   307→export const executeAgent = async params => {\n   308→  const { 332→    feedbackLines,\n   333→    forkActionsUrl,\n   334→    owner,\n   335→    repo,\n   336→    argv,\n   337→  });\n   338→\n   339→  // Build the system prompt\n   340→  const systemPrompt = 390→};\n   391→\n   392→export const executeAgentCommand = async params => {\n   393→  const { tempDir, branchName, prompt, systemPrompt, argv, log, formatAligned, getResourceSnapshot, forkedRepo, {\n   400→    // Execute agent command from the cloned repository directory\n   401→    if (retryCount === 0) {\n   402→      await log(`\\n${formatAligned('🤖', 'Executing Agent:',       "content": "#!/usr/bin/env node\n// Agent-related utility functions\n\n// Check if use is already defined (when imported from solve.mjs)\n// If not, fetch it (when running standalone)\nif (typeof globalThis.use === 'undefined') {\n  globalThis.use = (await eval(await (await fetch('https://unpkg.com/use-m/use.js')).text())).use;\n}\n\nconst { $ } = await use('command-stream');\nconst fs = (await use('fs')).promises;\nconst path = (await use('path')).default;\nconst os = (await use('os')).default;\n\n// Import log from general lib\nimport { log } from './lib.mjs';\nimport { from claude.lib.mjs\n// We reuse fetchModelInfo and checkModelVisionCapability to get data from models.dev API\nconst claudeLib = await import('./claude.lib.mjs');\nconst { fetchModelInfo, checkModelVisionCapability } = claudeLib;\n\n/**\n * Parse agent JSON output to extract token usage from step_finish events\n * Agent outputs NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON) with step_finish events containing token data\n * @param {string} output - Raw stdout output from agent command\n * @returns {Object} Aggregated token usage and cost data\n */\nexport const parseAgentTokenUsage = output => {\n  const usage = {\n    inputTokens: 0,\n    outputTokens: 0,\n    reasoningTokens: 0,\n    cacheReadTokens: 0,\n    cacheWriteTokens: 0,\n    totalCost: 0,\n    stepCount: 0,\n  };\n\n  // Try to parse each line as JSON (agent outputs NDJSON format)\n  const lines = output.split('\\n');\n  for (const line of lines) {\n    const trimmedLine = line.trim();\n    if (!trimmedLine || !trimmedLine.startsWith('{')) continue;\n\n    try {\n      const parsed = JSON.parse(trimmedLine);\n\n      // Look for step_finish events which contain token usage\n      if (parsed.type === 'step_finish' && parsed.part?.tokens) {\n        const tokens = parsed.part.tokens;\n        usage.stepCount++;\n\n        // Add token counts\n        if (tokens.input) usage.inputTokens += tokens.input;\n        if (tokens.output) usage.outputTokens += tokens.output;\n        if (tokens.reasoning) usage.reasoningTokens += tokens.reasoning;\n\n        // Handle cache tokens (can be in different formats)\n        if (tokens.cache) {\n          if (tokens.cache.read) usage.cacheReadTokens += tokens.cache.read;\n          if (tokens.cache.write) usage.cacheWriteTokens += tokens.cache.write;\n        }\n\n        // Add cost from step_finish (usually 0 for free models like grok-code)\n        if (parsed.part.cost !== undefined) {\n          usage.totalCost += parsed.part.cost;\n        }\n      }\n    } catch {\n      // Skip lines that aren't valid JSON\n      continue;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return usage;\n};\n\n/**\n * Helper function to get original provider name from provider identifier\n * Used for calculating public pricing estimates based on original provider prices\n * @param {string} providerId - Provider identifier (e.g., 'openai', 'anthropic', 'moonshot')\n * @returns {string} Human-readable provider name for pricing reference\n */\nconst getOriginalProviderName = providerId => {\n  if (!providerId) return null;\n\n  const providerMap = {\n    openai: 'OpenAI',\n    anthropic: 'Anthropic',\n    moonshot: 'Moonshot AI',\n    google: 'Google',\n    opencode: 'OpenCode Zen',\n    grok: 'xAI',\n  };\n\n  return providerMap[providerId] || providerId.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + providerId.slice(1);\n};\n\n/**\n * Calculate pricing for agent tool usage using models.dev API\n * Issue #1250: Shows actual provider (OpenCode Zen) and calculates public pricing estimate\n * based on original provider prices (Moonshot AI, OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)\n *\n * @param {string} modelId - The model ID used (e.g., 'opencode/grok-code')\n * @param {Object} tokenUsage - Token usage data from parseAgentTokenUsage\n * @returns {Object} Pricing information with:\n *   - provider: Always \"OpenCode Zen\" (actual provider)\n *   - originalProvider: The original model provider for pricing reference\n *   - totalCostUSD: Public pricing estimate based on original provider prices\n *   - opencodeCost: Actual billed cost from OpenCode Zen (free for most models)\n */\nexport const calculateAgentPricing = async (modelId, tokenUsage) => {\n  // Extract the model name from provider/model format\n  // e.g., 'opencode/grok-code' -> 'grok-code'\n  const modelName = modelId.includes('/') ? modelId.split('/').pop() : modelId;\n\n  // Extract provider from model ID to determine original provider for pricing\n  const providerFromModel = modelId.includes('/') ? modelId.split('/')[0] : null;\n\n  // Get original provider name for pricing reference\n  const originalProvider = getOriginalProviderName(providerFromModel);\n\n  try {\n    // Fetch model info from models.dev API\n    const modelInfo = await fetchModelInfo(modelName);\n\n    if (modelInfo && modelInfo.cost) {\n      const cost = modelInfo.cost;\n\n      // Calculate public pricing estimate based on original provider prices\n      // Prices are per 1M tokens, so divide by 1,000,000\n      const inputCost = (tokenUsage.inputTokens * (cost.input || 0)) / 1_000_000;\n      const outputCost = (tokenUsage.outputTokens * (cost.output || 0)) / 1_000_000;\n      const cacheReadCost = (tokenUsage.cacheReadTokens * (cost.cache_read || 0)) / 1_000_000;\n      const cacheWriteCost = (tokenUsage.cacheWriteTokens * (cost.cache_write || 0)) / 1_000_000;\n\n      const totalCost = inputCost + outputCost + cacheReadCost + cacheWriteCost;\n\n      // Determine if this is a free model from OpenCode Zen\n      // Models accessed via OpenCode Zen are free, regardless of original provider pricing\n      const isOpencodeFreeModel = providerFromModel === 'opencode' || modelName.toLowerCase().includes('free') || modelName.toLowerCase().includes('grok') || providerFromModel === 'moonshot' || providerFromModel === 'openai' || providerFromModel === 'anthropic';\n\n      return {\n        modelId,\n        modelName: modelInfo.name || modelName,\n        // Issue #1250: Always show OpenCode Zen as actual provider\n        provider: 'OpenCode Zen',\n        // Store original provider for reference in pricing display\n        originalProvider: originalProvider || modelInfo.provider || null,\n        pricing: {\n          inputPerMillion: cost.input || 0,\n          outputPerMillion: cost.output || 0,\n          cacheReadPerMillion: cost.cache_read || 0,\n          cacheWritePerMillion: cost.cache_write || 0,\n        },\n        tokenUsage,\n        breakdown: {\n          input: inputCost,\n          output: outputCost,\n          cacheRead: cacheReadCost,\n          cacheWrite: cacheWriteCost,\n        },\n        // Public pricing estimate based on original provider prices\n        totalCostUSD: totalCost,\n        // Actual cost from OpenCode Zen (free for supported models)\n        opencodeCost: isOpencodeFreeModel ? 0 : totalCost,\n        // Keep for backward compatibility - indicates if model has zero pricing\n        isFreeModel: cost.input === 0 && cost.output === 0,\n        // New flag to indicate if OpenCode Zen provides this model for free\n        isOpencodeFreeModel,\n      };\n    }\n    // Model not found in API, return what we have\n    return {\n      modelId,\n      modelName,\n      provider: 'OpenCode Zen',\n      originalProvider,\n      tokenUsage,\n      totalCostUSD: null,\n      opencodeCost: 0, // OpenCode Zen is free\n      isOpencodeFreeModel: true,\n      error: 'Model not found in models.dev API',\n    };\n  } catch (error) {\n    // Error fetching pricing, return with error info\n    return {\n      modelId,\n      modelName,\n      provider: 'OpenCode Zen',\n      originalProvider,\n      tokenUsage,\n      totalCostUSD: null,\n      opencodeCost: 0, // OpenCode Zen is free\n      isOpencodeFreeModel: true,\n      error: error.message,\n    };\n  }\n};\n\n// Model mapping to translate aliases to full model IDs for Agent\n// Agent uses OpenCode Zen's JSON interface and models\n// Issue #1185: Free models use opencode/ prefix (not openai/)\nexport const mapModelToId = model => {\n  const modelMap = {\n    grok: 'opencode/grok-code',\n    'grok-code': 'opencode/grok-code',\n    'grok-code-fast-1': 'opencode/grok-code',\n    'big-pickle': 'opencode/big-pickle',\n    'gpt-5-nano': 'opencode/gpt-5-nano',\n    sonnet: 'anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet',\n    haiku: 'anthropic/claude-3-5-haiku',\n    opus: 'anthropic/claude-3-opus',\n    'gemini-3-pro': 'google/gemini-3-pro',\n    // Free models mapping for issue #1250\n    'kimi-k2.5-free': 'moonshot/kimi-k2.5-free',\n    'gpt-4o-mini': 'openai/gpt-4o-mini',\n    'gpt-4o': 'openai/gpt-4o',\n    'claude-3.5-haiku': 'anthropic/claude-3.5-haiku',\n    'claude-3.5-sonnet': 'anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet',\n  };\n\n  // Return mapped model ID if it's an alias, otherwise return as-is\n  return modelMap[model] || model;\n};\n\n// Function to validate Agent connection\nexport const validateAgentConnection = async (model = 'grok-code-fast-1') => {\n  // Map model alias to full ID\n  const mappedModel = mapModelToId(model);\n\n  // Retry configuration\n  const maxRetries = 3;\n  let retryCount = 0;\n\n  const attemptValidation = async () => {\n    try {\n      if (retryCount === 0) {\n        await log('🔍 Validating Agent connection...');\n      } else {\n        await log(`🔄 Retry attempt ${retryCount}/${maxRetries} for Agent validation...`);\n      }\n\n      // Check if Agent CLI is installed and get version\n      try {\n        const versionResult = await $`timeout ${Math.floor(timeouts.opencodeCli / 1000)} agent --version`;\n        if (versionResult.code === 0) {\n          const version = versionResult.stdout?.toString().trim();\n          if (retryCount === 0) {\n            await log(`📦 Agent CLI version: ${version}`);\n          }\n        }\n      } catch (versionError) {\n        if (retryCount === 0) {\n          await log(`⚠️  Agent CLI version check failed (${versionError.code}), proceeding with connection test...`);\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Test basic Agent functionality with a simple \"hi\" message\n      // Agent uses the same JSON interface as OpenCode\n      const testResult = await $`printf \"hi\" | timeout ${Math.floor(timeouts.opencodeCli / 1000)} agent --model ${mappedModel}`;\n\n      if (testResult.code !== 0) {\n        const stderr = testResult.stderr?.toString() || '';\n\n        if (stderr.includes('auth') || stderr.includes('login')) {\n          await log('❌ Agent authentication failed', { level: 'error' });\n          await log('   💡 Note: Agent uses OpenCode models. For premium models, you may need: opencode auth', {\n            level: 'error',\n          });\n          return false;\n        }\n\n        await log(`❌ Agent validation failed with exit code ${testResult.code}`, { level: 'error' });\n        if (stderr) await log(`   Error: ${stderr.trim()}`, { level: 'error' });\n        return false;\n      }\n\n      // Success\n      await log('✅ Agent connection validated successfully');\n      return true;\n    } catch (error) {\n      await log(`❌ Failed to validate Agent connection: ${error.message}`, { level: 'error' });\n      await log('   💡 Make sure @link-assistant/agent is installed globally: bun install -g @link-assistant/agent', {\n        level: 'error',\n      });\n      return false;\n    }\n  };\n\n  // Start the validation\n  return await attemptValidation();\n};\n\n// Function to handle Agent runtime switching (if applicable)\nexport const handleAgentRuntimeSwitch = async () => {\n  // Agent is run via Bun as a CLI tool, runtime switching may not be applicable\n  // This function can be used for any runtime-specific configurations if needed\n  await log('ℹ️  Agent runtime handling not required for this operation');\n};\n\n// Main function to execute Agent with prompts and settings\nexport const executeAgent = async params => {\n  const { issueUrl, issueNumber, prNumber, prUrl, branchName, tempDir, workspaceTmpDir, isContinueMode, mergeStateStatus, forkedRepo, feedbackLines, forkActionsUrl, owner, repo, argv, log, formatAligned, getResourceSnapshot, agentPath = 'agent', $ } = params;\n\n  // Import prompt building functions from agent.prompts.lib.mjs\n  const { buildUserPrompt, buildSystemPrompt } = await import('./agent.prompts.lib.mjs');\n\n  // Check if the model supports vision using models.dev API\n  const mappedModel = mapModelToId(argv.model);\n  const modelSupportsVision = await checkModelVisionCapability(mappedModel);\n  if (argv.verbose) {\n    await log(`👁️  Model vision capability: ${modelSupportsVision ? 'supported' : 'not supported'}`, { verbose: true });\n  }\n\n  // Build the user prompt\n  const prompt = buildUserPrompt({\n    issueUrl,\n    issueNumber,\n    prNumber,\n    prUrl,\n    branchName,\n    tempDir,\n    workspaceTmpDir,\n    isContinueMode,\n    mergeStateStatus,\n    forkedRepo,\n    feedbackLines,\n    forkActionsUrl,\n    owner,\n    repo,\n    argv,\n  });\n\n  // Build the system prompt\n  const systemPrompt = buildSystemPrompt({\n    owner,\n    repo,\n    issueNumber,\n    prNumber,\n    branchName,\n    tempDir,\n    workspaceTmpDir,\n    isContinueMode,\n    forkedRepo,\n    argv,\n    modelSupportsVision,\n  });\n\n  // Log prompt details in verbose mode\n  if (argv.verbose) {\n    await log('\\n📝 Final prompt structure:', { verbose: true });\n    await log(`   Characters: ${prompt.length}`, { verbose: true });\n    await log(`   System prompt characters: ${systemPrompt.length}`, { verbose: true });\n    if (feedbackLines && feedbackLines.length > 0) {\n      await log('   Feedback info: Included', { verbose: true });\n    }\n\n    if (argv.dryRun) {\n      await log('\\n📋 User prompt content:', { verbose: true });\n      await log('---BEGIN USER PROMPT---', { verbose: true });\n      await log(prompt, { verbose: true });\n      await log('---END USER PROMPT---', { verbose: true });\n      await log('\\n📋 System prompt content:', { verbose: true });\n      await log('---BEGIN SYSTEM PROMPT---', { verbose: true });\n      await log(systemPrompt, { verbose: true });\n      await log('---END SYSTEM PROMPT---', { verbose: true });\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Execute the Agent command\n  return await executeAgentCommand({\n    tempDir,\n    branchName,\n    prompt,\n    systemPrompt,\n    argv,\n    log,\n    formatAligned,\n    getResourceSnapshot,\n    forkedRepo,\n    feedbackLines,\n    agentPath,\n    $,\n  });\n};\n\nexport const executeAgentCommand = async params => {\n  const { tempDir, branchName, prompt, systemPrompt, argv, log, formatAligned, getResourceSnapshot, forkedRepo, feedbackLines, agentPath, $ } = params;\n\n  // Retry configuration\n  const maxRetries = 3;\n  let retryCount = 0;\n\n  const executeWithRetry = async () => {\n    // Execute agent command from the cloned repository directory\n    if (retryCount === 0) {\n      await log(`\\n${formatAligned('🤖', 'Executing Agent:', argv.model.toUpperCase())}`);\n    } else {\n      await log(`\\n${formatAligned('🔄', 'Retry attempt:', `${retryCount}/${maxRetries}`)}`);\n    }\n\n    if (argv.verbose) {\n      await log(`   Model: ${argv.model}`, { verbose: true });\n      await log(`   Working directory: ${tempDir}`, { verbose: true });\n      await log(`   Branch: ${branchName}`, { verbose: true });\n      await log(`   Prompt length: ${prompt.length} chars`, { verbose: true });\n      await log(`   System prompt length: ${systemPrompt.length} chars`, { verbose: true });\n      if (feedbackLines && feedbackLines.length > 0) {\n        await log(`   Feedback info included: Yes (${feedbackLines.length} lines)`, { verbose: true });\n      } else {\n        await log('   Feedback info included: No', { verbose: true });\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Take resource snapshot before execution\n    const resourcesBefore = await getResourceSnapshot();\n    await log('📈 System resources before execution:', { verbose: true });\n    await log(`   Memory: ${resourcesBefore.memory.split('\\n')[1]}`, { verbose: true });\n    await log(`   Load: ${resourcesBefore.load}`, { verbose: true });\n\n    // Build Agent command\n    let execCommand;\n\n    // Map model alias to full ID\n    const mappedModel = mapModelToId(argv.model);\n\n    // Build agent command arguments\n    let agentArgs = `--model ${mappedModel}`;\n\n    // Propagate verbose flag to agent for detailed debugging output\n    if (argv.verbose) {\n      agentArgs += ' --verbose';\n    }\n\n    // Agent supports stdin in both plain text and JSON format\n    // We'll combine system and user prompts into a single message\n    const combinedPrompt = systemPrompt ? `${systemPrompt}\\n\\n${prompt}` : prompt;\n\n    // Write the combined prompt to a file for piping\n    // Use OS temporary directory instead of repository workspace to avoid polluting the repo\n    const promptFile = path.join(os.tmpdir(), `agent_prompt_${Date.now()}_${process.pid}.txt`);\n    await fs.writeFile(promptFile, combinedPrompt);\n\n    // Build the full command - pipe the prompt file to agent\n    const fullCommand = `(cd \"${tempDir}\" && cat \"${promptFile}\" | ${agentPath} ${agentArgs})`;\n\n    await log(`\\n${formatAligned('📝', 'Raw command:', '')}`);\n    await log(`${fullCommand}`);\n    await log('');\n\n    try {\n      // Pipe the prompt file to agent via stdin\n      // Use agentArgs which includes --model and optionally --verbose\n      execCommand = $({\n        cwd: tempDir,\n        mirror: false,\n      })`cat ${promptFile} | ${agentPath} ${agentArgs}`;\n\n      await log(`${formatAligned('📋', 'Command details:', '')}`);\n      await log(formatAligned('📂', 'Working directory:', tempDir, 2));\n      await log(formatAligned('🌿', 'Branch:', branchName, 2));\n      await log(formatAligned('🤖', 'Model:', `Agent ${argv.model.toUpperCase()}`, 2));\n      if (argv.fork && forkedRepo) {\n        await log(formatAligned('🍴', 'Fork:', forkedRepo, 2));\n      }\n\n      await log(`\\n${formatAligned('▶️', 'Streaming output:', '')}\\n`);\n\n      let exitCode = 0;\n      let sessionId = null;\n      let limitReached = false;\n      let limitResetTime = null;\n      let lastMessage = '';\n      let fullOutput = ''; // Collect all output for pricing calculation and error detection\n      // Issue #1201: Track error events detected during streaming for reliable error detection\n      // Post-hoc detection on fullOutput can miss errors if NDJSON lines get concatenated without newlines\n      let streamingErrorDetected = false;\n      let streamingErrorMessage = null;\n\n      for await (const chunk of execCommand.stream()) {\n        if (chunk.type === 'stdout') {\n          const output = chunk.data.toString();\n          // Split output into individual lines for NDJSON parsing\n          // Agent outputs NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON) format where each line is a separate JSON object\n          // This allows us to parse each event independently and extract structured data like session IDs\n          const lines = output.split('\\n');\n          for (const line of lines) {\n            if (!line.trim()) continue;\n            try {\n              const data = JSON.parse(line);\n              // Output formatted JSON\n              await log(JSON.stringify(data, null, 2));\n              // Capture session ID from the first message\n              if (!sessionId && data.sessionID) {\n                sessionId = data.sessionID;\n                await log(`📌 Session ID: ${sessionId}`);\n              }\n              // Issue #1201: Detect error events during streaming for reliable detection\n              if (data.type === 'error' || data.type === 'step_error') {\n                streamingErrorDetected = true;\n                streamingErrorMessage = data.message || data.error || line.substring(0, 100);\n                await log(`⚠️  Error event detected in stream: ${streamingErrorMessage}`, { level: 'warning' });\n              }\n            } catch {\n              // Not JSON - log as plain text\n              await log(line);\n            }\n          }\n          lastMessage = output;\n          fullOutput += output; // Collect for both pricing calculation and error detection\n        }\n\n        if (chunk.type === 'stderr') {\n          const errorOutput = chunk.data.toString();\n          if (errorOutput) {\n            // Agent sends all output (including verbose logs and structured events) to stderr\n            // Process each line as NDJSON, same as stdout handling\n            const stderrLines = errorOutput.split('\\n');\n            for (const stderrLine of stderrLines) {\n              if (!stderrLine.trim()) continue;\n              try {\n                const stderrData = JSON.parse(stderrLine);\n                // Output formatted JSON (same formatting as stdout)\n                await log(JSON.stringify(stderrData, null, 2));\n                // Capture session ID from stderr too (agent sends it via stderr)\n                if (!sessionId && stderrData.sessionID) {\n                  sessionId = stderrData.sessionID;\n                  await log(`📌 Session ID: ${sessionId}`);\n                }\n                // Issue #1201: Detect error events during streaming (stderr) for reliable detection\n                if (stderrData.type === 'error' || stderrData.type === 'step_error') {\n                  streamingErrorDetected = true;\n                  streamingErrorMessage = stderrData.message || stderrData.error || stderrLine.substring(0, 100);\n                  await log(`⚠️  Error event detected in stream: ${streamingErrorMessage}`, { level: 'warning' });\n                }\n              } catch {\n                // Not JSON - log as plain text\n                await log(stderrLine);\n              }\n            }\n            // Also collect stderr for error detection\n            fullOutput += errorOutput;\n          }\n        } else if (chunk.type === 'exit') {\n          exitCode = chunk.code;\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Simplified error detection for agent tool\n      // Issue #886: Trust exit code - agent now properly returns code 1 on errors with JSON error response\n      // Don't scan output for error patterns as this causes false positives during normal operation\n      // (e.g., AI executing bash commands that produce \"Permission denied\" warnings but succeed)\n      //\n      // Error detection is now based on:\n      // 1. Non-zero exit code (agent returns 1 on errors)\n      // 2. Explicit JSON error messages from agent (type: \"error\")\n      // 3. Usage limit detection (handled separately)\n      const detectAgentErrors = stdoutOutput => {\n        const lines = stdoutOutput.split('\\n');\n\n        for (const line of lines) {\n          if (!line.trim()) continue;\n\n          try {\n            const msg = JSON.parse(line);\n\n            // Check for explicit error message types from agent\n            if (msg.type === 'error' || msg.type === 'step_error') {\n              return { detected: true, type: 'AgentError', match: msg.message || msg.error || line.substring(0, 100) };\n            }\n          } catch {\n            // Not JSON - ignore for error detection\n            continue;\n          }\n        }\n\n        return { detected: false };\n      };\n\n      // Only check for JSON error messages, not pattern matching in output\n      const outputError = detectAgentErrors(fullOutput);\n\n      // Issue #1201: Use streaming detection as primary, post-hoc as fallback\n      // Streaming detection is more reliable because it parses each JSON line as it arrives,\n      // avoiding issues where NDJSON lines get concatenated without newline delimiters in fullOutput\n      if (!outputError.detected && streamingErrorDetected) {\n        outputError.detected = true;\n        outputError.type = 'AgentError';\n        outputError.match = streamingErrorMessage;\n      }\n\n      if (exitCode !== 0 || outputError.detected) {\n        // Build JSON error structure for consistent error reporting\n        const errorInfo = {\n          type: 'error',\n          exitCode,\n          errorDetectedInOutput: outputError.detected,\n          errorType: outputError.detected ? outputError.type : exitCode !== 0 ? 'NonZeroExitCode' : null,\n          errorMatch: outputError.detected ? outputError.match : null,\n          message: null,\n          sessionId,\n          limitReached: false,\n          limitResetTime: null,\n        };\n\n        // Check for usage limit errors first (more specific)\n        const limitInfo = detectUsageLimit(lastMessage);\n        if (limitInfo.isUsageLimit) {\n          limitReached = true;\n          limitResetTime = limitInfo.resetTime;\n          errorInfo.limitReached = true;\n          errorInfo.limitResetTime = limitResetTime;\n          errorInfo.errorType = 'UsageLimit';\n\n          // Format and display user-friendly message\n          const messageLines = formatUsageLimitMessage({\n            tool: 'Agent',\n            resetTime: limitInfo.resetTime,\n            sessionId,\n            resumeCommand: sessionId ? `${process.argv[0]} ${process.argv[1]} ${argv.url} --resume ${sessionId}` : null,\n          });\n\n          for (const line of messageLines) {\n            await log(line, { level: 'warning' });\n          }\n        } else if (outputError.detected) {\n          // Explicit JSON error message from agent (Issue #1201: includes streaming-detected errors)\n          errorInfo.message = `Agent reported error: ${outputError.match}`;\n          await log(`\\n\\n❌ ${errorInfo.message}`, { level: 'error' });\n        } else {\n          errorInfo.message = `Agent command failed with exit code ${exitCode}`;\n          await log(`\\n\\n❌ ${errorInfo.message}`, { level: 'error' });\n        }\n\n        // Log error as JSON for structured output (since agent expects JSON input/output)\n        await log('\\n📋 Error details (JSON):', { level: 'error' });\n        await log(JSON.stringify(errorInfo, null, 2), { level: 'error' });\n\n        const resourcesAfter = await getResourceSnapshot();\n        await log('\\n📈 System resources after execution:', { verbose: true });\n        await log(`   Memory: ${resourcesAfter.memory.split('\\n')[1]}`, { verbose: true });\n        await log(`   Load: ${resourcesAfter.load}`, { verbose: true });\n\n        // Parse token usage even on failure (partial work may have been done)\n        const tokenUsage = parseAgentTokenUsage(fullOutput);\n        const pricingInfo = await calculateAgentPricing(mappedModel, tokenUsage);\n\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          sessionId,\n          limitReached,\n          limitResetTime,\n          errorInfo, // Include structured error information\n          tokenUsage,\n          pricingInfo,\n          publicPricingEstimate: pricingInfo.totalCostUSD,\n        };\n      }\n\n      await log('\\n\\n✅ Agent command completed');\n\n      // Parse token usage from collected output\n      const tokenUsage = parseAgentTokenUsage(fullOutput);\n      const pricingInfo = await calculateAgentPricing(mappedModel, tokenUsage);\n\n      // Log pricing information\n      if (tokenUsage.stepCount > 0) {\n        await log('\\n💰 Token Usage Summary:');\n        await log(`   📊 ${pricingInfo.modelName || mappedModel}:`);\n        await log(`      Input tokens: ${tokenUsage.inputTokens.toLocaleString()}`);\n        await log(`      Output tokens: ${tokenUsage.outputTokens.toLocaleString()}`);\n        if (tokenUsage.reasoningTokens > 0) {\n          await log(`      Reasoning tokens: ${tokenUsage.reasoningTokens.toLocaleString()}`);\n        }\n        if (tokenUsage.cacheReadTokens > 0 || tokenUsage.cacheWriteTokens > 0) {\n          await log(`      Cache read: ${tokenUsage.cacheReadTokens.toLocaleString()}`);\n          await log(`      Cache write: ${tokenUsage.cacheWriteTokens.toLocaleString()}`);\n        }\n\n        if (pricingInfo.totalCostUSD !== null) {\n          if (pricingInfo.isFreeModel) {\n            await log('      Cost: $0.00 (Free model)');\n          } else {\n            await log(`      Cost: $${pricingInfo.totalCostUSD.toFixed(6)}`);\n          }\n          await log(`      Provider: ${pricingInfo.provider || 'OpenCode Zen'}`);\n        } else {\n          await log('      Cost: Not available (could not fetch pricing)');\n        }\n      }\n\n      return {\n        success: true,\n        sessionId,\n        limitReached,\n        limitResetTime,\n        tokenUsage,\n        pricingInfo,\n        publicPricingEstimate: pricingInfo.totalCostUSD,\n      };\n    } catch (error) {\n      reportError(error, {\n        context: 'execute_agent',\n        command: params.command,\n        agentPath: params.agentPath,\n        operation: 'run_agent_command',\n      });\n\n      await log(`\\n\\n❌ Error executing Agent command: ${error.message}`, { level: 'error' });\n      return {\n        success: false,\n        sessionId: null,\n        limitReached: false,\n        limitResetTime: null,\n        tokenUsage: null,\n        pricingInfo: null,\n        publicPricingEstimate: null,\n      };\n    }\n  };\n\n  // Start the execution with retry logic\n  return await executeWithRetry();\n};\n\nexport const checkForUncommittedChanges = async (tempDir, owner, repo, branchName, $, log, autoCommit = false, autoRestartEnabled = true) => {\n  // Similar to OpenCode version, check for uncommitted changes\n  await log('\\n🔍 Checking for uncommitted changes...');\n  try {\n    const gitStatusResult = await $({ cwd: tempDir })`git status --porcelain 2>&1`;\n\n    if (gitStatusResult.code === 0) {\n      const statusOutput = gitStatusResult.stdout.toString().trim();\n\n      if (statusOutput) {\n        await log('📝 Found uncommitted changes');\n        await log('Changes:');\n        for (const line of statusOutput.split('\\n')) {\n          await log(`   ${line}`);\n        }\n\n        if (autoCommit) {\n          await log('💾 Auto-committing changes (--auto-commit-uncommitted-changes is enabled)...');\n\n          const addResult = await $({ cwd: tempDir })`git add -A`;\n          if (addResult.code === 0) {\n            const commitMessage = 'Auto-commit: Changes made by Agent during problem-solving session';\n            const commitResult = await $({ cwd: tempDir })`git commit -m ${commitMessage}`;\n\n            if (commitResult.code === 0) {\n              await log('✅ Changes committed successfully');\n\n              const pushResult = await $({ cwd: tempDir })`git push origin ${branchName}`;\n\n              if (pushResult.code === 0) {\n                await log('✅ Changes pushed successfully');\n              } else {\n                await log(`⚠️ Warning: Could not push changes: ${pushResult.stderr?.toString().trim()}`, {\n                  level: 'warning',\n                });\n              }\n            } else {\n              await log(`⚠️ Warning: Could not commit changes: ${commitResult.stderr?.toString().trim()}`, {\n                level: 'warning',\n              });\n            }\n          } else {\n            await log(`⚠️ Warning: Could not stage changes: ${addResult.stderr?.toString().trim()}`, {\n              level: 'warning',\n            });\n          }\n          return false;\n        } else if (autoRestartEnabled) {\n          await log('');\n          await log('⚠️  IMPORTANT: Uncommitted changes detected!');\n          await log('   Agent made changes that were not committed.');\n          await log('');\n          await log('🔄 AUTO-RESTART: Restarting Agent to handle uncommitted changes...');\n          await log('   Agent will review the changes and decide what to commit.');\n          await log('');\n          return true;\n        } else {\n          await log('');\n          await log('⚠️  Uncommitted changes detected but auto-restart is disabled.');\n          await log('   Use --auto-restart-on-uncommitted-changes to enable or commit manually.');\n          await log('');\n          return false;\n        }\n      } else {\n        await log('✅ No uncommitted changes found');\n        return false;\n      }\n    } else {\n      await log(`⚠️ Warning: Could not check git status: ${gitStatusResult.stderr?.toString().trim()}`, {\n        level: 'warning',\n      });\n      return false;\n    }\n  } catch (gitError) {\n    reportError(gitError, {\n      context: 'check_uncommitted_changes_agent',\n      tempDir,\n      operation: 'git_status_check',\n    });\n    await log(`⚠️ Warning: Error checking for uncommitted changes: ${gitError.message}`, { level: 'warning' });\n    return false;\n  }\n};\n\n// Export all functions as default object too\nexport default {\n  validateAgentConnection,\n  handleAgentRuntimeSwitch,\n  executeAgent,\n  executeAgentCommand,\n  checkForUncommittedChanges,\n  parseAgentTokenUsage,\n  calculateAgentPricing,\n};\n",
      "numLines": 825,
      "startLine": 1,
      "totalLines": 825
    }
  }

Raw stdout output from agent command\n * @returns {Object} Aggregated token usage and cost data\n */\nexport const parseAgentTokenUsage = output => {\n  const usage = {\n    inputTokens: 0,\n    outputTokens: 0,\n    reasoningTokens: 0,\n    cacheReadTokens: 0,\n    cacheWriteTokens: 0,\n    totalCost: 0,\n    stepCount: 0,\n  };\n\n  // Try to parse each line as JSON (agent outputs NDJSON format)\n  const lines = output.split('\\n');\n  for (const line of lines) {\n    const trimmedLine = line.trim();\n    if (!trimmedLine || !trimmedLine.startsWith('{')) continue;\n\n    try {\n      const parsed = JSON.parse(trimmedLine);\n\n      // Look for step_finish events which contain token usage\n      if (parsed.type === 'step_finish' && parsed.part?.tokens) {\n        const tokens = parsed.part.tokens;\n        usage.stepCount++;\n\n        // Add token counts\n        if (tokens.input) usage.inputTokens += tokens.input;\n        if (tokens.output) usage.outputTokens += tokens.output;\n        if (tokens.reasoning) usage.reasoningTokens += tokens.reasoning;\n\n        // Handle cache tokens (can be in different formats)\n        if (tokens.cache) {\n          if (tokens.cache.read) usage.cacheReadTokens += tokens.cache.read;\n          if (tokens.cache.write) usage.cacheWriteTokens += tokens.cache.write;\n        }\n\n        // Add cost from step_finish (usually 0 for free models like grok-code)\n        if (parsed.part.cost !== undefined) {\n          usage.totalCost += parsed.part.cost;\n        }\n      }\n    } catch {\n      // Skip lines that aren't valid JSON\n      continue;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return usage;\n};\n\n/**\n * Helper function to get original provider name from provider identifier\n * Used for calculating public pricing estimates based on original provider prices\n * @param {string} providerId - (diff / totalCostUSD) * 100 : 0;\n    66→      costInfo += `\\n- Difference: $${diff.toFixed(6)} (${pct > 0 ? '+' : ''}${pct.toFixed(2)}%)`;\n    67→    }\n    68→  }\n    69→  return costInfo;\n    70→};\n    71→\n    72→// Test framework\n    73→let testsPassed = 0;\n    74→let testsFailed = 0;\n    75→\n 94→function assertContains(str, substring, message = '') {\n    95→  if (!str.includes(substring)) {\n    96→    throw new Error(`${message}\\nExpected string to contain: \"${substring}\"\\nActual       "content": "#!/usr/bin/env node\n\n/**\n * Unit tests for buildCostInfoString function\n * Tests the cost information formatting logic for Issue #1015\n *\n * This function builds cost estimation strings for log comments.\n * Key behaviors tested:\n * - Returns empty string when all values are unknown (Issue #1015 Bug 2)\n * - Properly formats public pricing estimates\n * - Properly formats Anthropic calculated costs\n * - Shows difference when both values are available\n * - Handles pricing info with model name, provider, token usage\n * - Handles free model special case\n */\n\n// Copy of the buildCostInfoString function from src/github.lib.mjs for testing\n// This allows us to test the function in isolation without requiring the full module dependencies\n// Issue #1250: Enhanced to support OpenCode Zen cost and original provider reference\nconst buildCostInfoString = (totalCostUSD, anthropicTotalCostUSD, pricingInfo) => {\n  // Issue #1015: Don't show cost section when all values are unknown (clutters output)\n  const hasPublic = totalCostUSD !== null && totalCostUSD !== undefined;\n  const hasAnthropic = anthropicTotalCostUSD !== null && anthropicTotalCostUSD !== undefined;\n  const hasPricing = pricingInfo && (pricingInfo.modelName || pricingInfo.tokenUsage || pricingInfo.isFreeModel || pricingInfo.isOpencodeFreeModel);\n  // Issue #1250: Check for OpenCode Zen actual cost\n  const hasOpencodeCost = pricingInfo?.opencodeCost !== null && pricingInfo?.opencodeCost !== undefined;\n  if (!hasPublic && !hasAnthropic && !hasPricing && !hasOpencodeCost) return '';\n  let costInfo = '\\n\\n💰 **Cost estimation:**';\n  if (pricingInfo?.modelName) {\n    costInfo += `\\n- Model: ${pricingInfo.modelName}`;\n    if (pricingInfo.provider) costInfo += `\\n- Provider: ${pricingInfo.provider}`;\n  }\n  // Issue #1250: Show public pricing estimate based on original provider prices\n  if (hasPublic) {\n    // For models with zero pricing from original provider, show as free\n    if (pricingInfo?.isFreeModel && totalCostUSD === 0) {\n      costInfo += '\\n- Public pricing estimate: $0.00 (Free model)';\n    } else {\n      // Show actual public pricing estimate with original provider reference\n      const originalProviderRef = pricingInfo?.originalProvider ? ` (based on ${pricingInfo.originalProvider} prices)` : '';\n      costInfo += `\\n- Public pricing estimate: $${totalCostUSD.toFixed(6)}${originalProviderRef}`;\n    }\n  } else if (hasPricing) {\n    costInfo += '\\n- Public pricing estimate: unknown';\n  }\n  // Issue #1250: Show actual cost from OpenCode Zen for agent tool\n  if (hasOpencodeCost) {\n    if (pricingInfo.isOpencodeFreeModel) {\n      costInfo += '\\n- Calculated by OpenCode Zen: $0.00 (Free model)';\n    } else {\n      costInfo += `\\n- Calculated by OpenCode Zen: $${pricingInfo.opencodeCost.toFixed(6)}`;\n    }\n  }\n  if (pricingInfo?.tokenUsage) {\n    const u = pricingInfo.tokenUsage;\n    let tokenInfo = `\\n- Token usage: ${u.inputTokens?.toLocaleString() || 0} input, ${u.outputTokens?.toLocaleString() || 0} output`;\n    if (u.reasoningTokens > 0) tokenInfo += `, ${u.reasoningTokens.toLocaleString()} reasoning`;\n    if (u.cacheReadTokens > 0 || u.cacheWriteTokens > 0) tokenInfo += `, ${u.cacheReadTokens?.toLocaleString() || 0} cache read, ${u.cacheWriteTokens?.toLocaleString() || 0} cache write`;\n    costInfo += tokenInfo;\n  }\n  if (hasAnthropic) {\n    costInfo += `\\n- Calculated by Anthropic: $${anthropicTotalCostUSD.toFixed(6)} USD`;\n    if (hasPublic) {\n      const diff = anthropicTotalCostUSD - totalCostUSD;\n      const pct = totalCostUSD > 0 ? (diff / totalCostUSD) * 100 : 0;\n      costInfo += `\\n- Difference: $${diff.toFixed(6)} (${pct > 0 ? '+' : ''}${pct.toFixed(2)}%)`;\n    }\n  }\n  return costInfo;\n};\n\n// Test framework\nlet testsPassed = 0;\nlet testsFailed = 0;\n\nfunction runTest(name, testFn) {\n  process.stdout.write(`Testing ${name}... `);\n  try {\n    testFn();\n    console.log('✅ PASSED');\n    testsPassed++;\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.log(`❌ FAILED: ${error.message}`);\n    testsFailed++;\n  }\n}\n\nfunction assertEqual(actual, expected, message = '') {\n  if (actual !== expected) {\n    throw new Error(`${message}\\nExpected: ${JSON.stringify(expected)}\\nActual: ${JSON.stringify(actual)}`);\n  }\n}\n\nfunction assertContains(str, substring, message = '') {\n  if (!str.includes(substring)) {\n    throw new Error(`${message}\\nExpected string to contain: \"${substring}\"\\nActual string: \"${str}\"`);\n  }\n}\n\nfunction assertNotContains(str, substring, message = '') {\n  if (str.includes(substring)) {\n    throw new Error(`${message}\\nExpected string NOT to contain: \"${substring}\"\\nActual string: \"${str}\"`);\n  }\n}\n\nconsole.log('🧪 Running buildCostInfoString unit tests (Issue #1015)...\\n');\nconsole.log('='.repeat(80));\n\n// ==== Issue #1015 Bug 2: Empty when all values unknown ====\nconsole.log('\\n📋 Test Group: Issue #1015 Bug 2 - Empty when all values unknown\\n');\n\nrunTest('returns empty string when all values are null', () => {\n  const result = buildCostInfoString(null, null, null);\n  assertEqual(result, '', 'Should return empty string when all values are null');\n});\n\nrunTest('returns empty string when all values are undefined', () => {\n  const result = buildCostInfoString(undefined, undefined, undefined);\n  assertEqual(result, '', 'Should return empty string when all values are undefined');\n});\n\nrunTest('returns empty string with empty pricing info object', () => {\n  const result = buildCostInfoString(null, null, {});\n  assertEqual(result, '', 'Should return empty string with empty pricing info');\n});\n\nrunTest('returns empty string when pricing info has no relevant fields', () => {\n  const result = buildCostInfoString(null, null, { irrelevantField: 'value' });\n  assertEqual(result, '', 'Should return empty string when pricing info has no relevant fields');\n});\n\n// ==== Public pricing estimate tests ====\nconsole.log('\\n📋 Test Group: Public pricing estimate\\n');\n\nrunTest('shows public pricing estimate when available', () => {\n  const result = buildCostInfoString(1.5, null, null);\n  assertContains(result, '💰 **Cost estimation:**', 'Should contain cost estimation header');\n  assertContains(result, 'Public pricing estimate: $1.500000', 'Should contain formatted cost');\n});\n\nrunTest('formats small cost with 6 decimal places', () => {\n  const result = buildCostInfoString(0.000123, null, null);\n  assertContains(result, '$0.000123', 'Should format with 6 decimal places');\n});\n\nrunTest('formats zero cost correctly', () => {\n  const result = buildCostInfoString(0, null, null);\n  assertContains(result, '$0.000000', 'Should format zero with 6 decimal places');\n});\n\n// ==== Anthropic cost tests ====\nconsole.log('\\n📋 Test Group: Anthropic calculated cost\\n');\n\nrunTest('shows Anthropic calculated cost when available', () => {\n  const result = buildCostInfoString(null, 2.5, null);\n  assertContains(result, 'Calculated by Anthropic: $2.500000 USD', 'Should show Anthropic cost');\n});\n\nrunTest('shows difference when both costs available (Anthropic higher)', () => {\n  const result = buildCostInfoString(1.0, 1.5, null);\n  assertContains(result, 'Difference: $0.500000 (+50.00%)', 'Should show positive difference');\n});\n\nrunTest('shows difference when both costs available (Anthropic lower)', () => {\n  const result = buildCostInfoString(2.0, 1.5, null);\n  assertContains(result, 'Difference: $-0.500000 (-25.00%)', 'Should show negative difference');\n});\n\nrunTest('shows zero difference when costs are equal', () => {\n  const result = buildCostInfoString(1.0, 1.0, null);\n  assertContains(result, 'Difference: $0.000000 (0.00%)', 'Should show zero difference');\n});\n\nrunTest('handles zero public cost in percentage calculation', () => {\n  const result = buildCostInfoString(0, 1.0, null);\n  assertContains(result, 'Difference: $1.000000 (0.00%)', 'Should handle division by zero');\n});\n\n// ==== Pricing info tests ====\nconsole.log('\\n📋 Test Group: Pricing info formatting\\n');\n\nrunTest('shows model name when available', () => {\n  const result = buildCostInfoString(null, null, { modelName: 'claude-3-opus' });\n  assertContains(result, 'Model: claude-3-opus', 'Should show model name');\n});\n\nrunTest('shows provider when model name and provider available', () => {\n  const result = buildCostInfoString(null, null, {\n    modelName: 'gpt-4',\n    provider: 'OpenAI',\n  });\n  assertContains(result, 'Model: gpt-4', 'Should show model name');\n  assertContains(result, 'Provider: OpenAI', 'Should show provider');\n});\n\nrunTest('shows unknown for public pricing when has pricing info but no public cost', () => {\n  const result = buildCostInfoString(null, null, { modelName: 'claude-3' });\n  assertContains(result, 'Public pricing estimate: unknown', 'Should show assertContains(result, 'Public pricing estimate: $0.00 (Free model)', 'Should show free model');\n});\n\n// ==== Token usage tests ====\nconsole.log('\\n📋 Test Group: Token usage 0,\n      cacheWriteTokens: 0,\n    },\n  });\n  assertNotContains(result, 'cache', 'Should not show zero cache tokens');\n});\n\n// ==== Comprehensive format tests ====\nconsole.log('\\n📋 Test Group:           "file_path": "/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1770844904353/experiments/test-issue-1250-pricing.mjs",
          "content": "#!/usr/bin/env node\n\n/**\n * Test script for Issue #1250: Fix `--tool agent` pricing display\n *\n * This experiment verifies:\n * 1. How model names are mapped from \"kimi-k2.5-free\" to find pricing in models.dev\n * 2. How token usage parsing works with step_finish events\n */\n\n// Use the same setup as the actual code\nif (typeof globalThis.use === 'undefined') {\n  globalThis.use = (await eval(await (await fetch('https://unpkg.com/use-m/use.js')).text())).use;\n}\n\nconst https = (await use('https')).default;\n\nconsole.log('🧪 Issue #1250 Pricing Display Test\\n');\nconsole.log('='.repeat(80));\n\n// Test 1: Model name mapping\nconsole.log('\\n📋 Test 1: Model Name Mapping\\n');\n\nconst testModels = [\n  'kimi-k2.5-free',\n  'moonshot/kimi-k2.5-free',\n  'opencode/grok-code',\n  'openai/gpt-4o-mini',\n  'anthropic/claude-3.5-haiku',\n];\n\nfor (const modelId of testModels) {\n  const modelName = modelId.includes('/') ? modelId.split('/').pop() : modelId;\n  console.log(`  \"${modelId}\" -> modelName: \"${modelName}\"`);\n}\n\n// Test 2: Fetch models.dev API and check if kimi-k2.5-free exists\nconsole.log('\\n📋 Test 2: Check models.dev API for Kimi models\\n');\n\nconst fetchModelsApi = () => {\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n    https.get('https://models.dev/api.json', res => {\n      let data = '';\n      res.on('data', chunk => { data += chunk; });\n      res.on('end', () => {\n        try {\n          resolve(JSON.parse(data));\n        } catch (e) {\n          reject(e);\n        }\n      });\n    }).on('error', reject);\n  });\n};\n\ntry {\n  const apiData = await fetchModelsApi();\n\n  // Check for kimi models\n  const kimiModels = [];\n  for (const [providerKey, provider] of Object.entries(apiData)) {\n    if (provider.models) {\n      for (const [modelId, modelInfo] of Object.entries(provider.models)) {\n        if (modelId.toLowerCase().includes('kimi')) {\n          kimiModels.push({\n            provider: provider.name || providerKey,\n            modelId,\n            cost: modelInfo.cost,\n          });\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  console.log('  Kimi models found in API:');\n  for (const model of kimiModels.slice(0, 10)) {\n    console.log(`    - ${model.provider} / ${model.modelId}`);\n    if (model.cost) {\n      console.log(`      Cost: $${model.cost.input}/1M input, $${model.cost.output}/1M output`);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Test specific lookups\n  console.log('\\n  Testing specific model lookups:');\n\n  const testLookups = ['kimi-k2.5-free', 'kimi-k2.5', 'kimi-k2-0905-preview'];\n  for (const modelId of testLookups) {\n    let found = false;\n    for (const [providerKey, provider] of Object.entries(apiData)) {\n      if (provider.models && provider.models[modelId]) {\n        found = true;\n        const info = provider.models[modelId];\n        console.log(`    ✅ \"${modelId}\" found in ${provider.name || providerKey}`);\n        console.log(`       Name: ${info.name}, Cost: $${info.cost?.input || '?'} input, $${info.cost?.output || '?'} output`);\n        break;\n      }\n    }\n    if (!found) {\n      console.log(`    ❌ \"${modelId}\" NOT FOUND in any provider`);\n    }\n  }\n\n} catch (error) {\n  console.log('  Error fetching API:', error.message);\n}\n\n// Test 3: Token parsing simulation\nconsole.log('\\n📋 Test 3: Token Usage Parsing Test\\n');\n\n// Simulate step_finish events as they appear in the output\nconst sampleOutput = `{\"type\":\"status\",\"mode\":\"stdin-stream\"}\n{\"type\":\"session.created\",\"sessionID\":\"ses_test123\"}\n{\"type\":\"step_finish\",\"timestamp\":1770842531248,\"sessionID\":\"ses_test123\",\"part\":{\"id\":\"prt_test1\",\"type\":\"step-finish\",\"cost\":0,\"tokens\":{\"input\":15413,\"output\":64,\"reasoning\":0,\"cache\":{\"read\":32,\"write\":0}}}}\n{\"type\":\"step_finish\",\"timestamp\":1770842551248,\"sessionID\":\"ses_test123\",\"part\":{\"id\":\"prt_test2\",\"type\":\"step-finish\",\"cost\":0,\"tokens\":{\"input\":25,\"output\":43,\"reasoning\":1,\"cache\":{\"read\":68045,\"write\":0}}}}\n`;\n\n// This is the parseAgentTokenUsage function copy\nconst parseAgentTokenUsage = output => {\n  const usage = {\n    inputTokens: 0,\n    outputTokens: 0,\n    reasoningTokens: 0,\n    cacheReadTokens: 0,\n    cacheWriteTokens: 0,\n    totalCost: 0,\n    stepCount: 0,\n  };\n\n  const lines = output.split('\\n');\n  for (const line of lines) {\n    const trimmedLine = line.trim();\n    if (!trimmedLine || !trimmedLine.startsWith('{')) continue;\n\n    try {\n      const parsed = JSON.parse(trimmedLine);\n\n      if (parsed.type === 'step_finish' && parsed.part?.tokens) {\n        const tokens = parsed.part.tokens;\n        usage.stepCount++;\n\n        if (tokens.input) usage.inputTokens += tokens.input;\n        if (tokens.output) usage.outputTokens += tokens.output;\n        if (tokens.reasoning) usage.reasoningTokens += tokens.reasoning;\n\n        if (tokens.cache) {\n          if (tokens.cache.read) usage.cacheReadTokens += tokens.cache.read;\n          if (tokens.cache.write) usage.cacheWriteTokens += tokens.cache.write;\n        }\n\n        if (parsed.part.cost !== undefined) {\n          usage.totalCost += parsed.part.cost;\n        }\n      }\n    } catch {\n      continue;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return usage;\n};\n\nconst tokenUsage = parseAgentTokenUsage(sampleOutput);\nconsole.log('  Sample output parsing result:');\nconsole.log(`    Step count: ${tokenUsage.stepCount}`);\nconsole.log(`    Input tokens: ${tokenUsage.inputTokens}`);\nconsole.log(`    Output tokens: ${tokenUsage.outputTokens}`);\nconsole.log(`    Reasoning tokens: ${tokenUsage.reasoningTokens}`);\nconsole.log(`    Cache read tokens: ${tokenUsage.cacheReadTokens}`);\nconsole.log(`    Cache write tokens: ${tokenUsage.cacheWriteTokens}`);\n\n// Test with empty/problematic output\nconsole.log('\\n  Testing edge cases:');\nconsole.log(`    Empty string: ${JSON.stringify(parseAgentTokenUsage(''))}`);\nconsole.log(`    No step_finish events: ${JSON.stringify(parseAgentTokenUsage('{\"type\":\"text\",\"content\":\"hello\"}'))}`);\n\n// Test 4: Proposed fix - normalize model names\nconsole.log('\\n📋 Test 4: Proposed Model Name Normalization\\n');\n\n/**\n * Normalize model name to match models.dev API format\n * - Remove \"-free\" suffix for pricing lookup\n * - Handle aliases\n */\nconst normalizeModelForPricing = (modelId) => {\n  // Extract model name from provider/model format\n  let modelName = modelId.includes('/') ? modelId.split('/').pop() : modelId;\n\n  // Map of model aliases to their paid equivalents in models.dev\n  const modelAliasMap = {\n    // Free versions map to their paid equivalents\n    'kimi-k2.5-free': 'kimi-k2.5',\n    'grok-code': 'grok-3', // Grok code might map to grok-3 or similar\n    'gpt-4o-mini-free': 'gpt-4o-mini',\n    'claude-3.5-haiku-free': 'claude-3-5-haiku-20241022',\n    // Add more mappings as needed\n  };\n\n  // Check if there's a direct alias\n  if (modelAliasMap[modelName]) {\n    return modelAliasMap[modelName];\n  }\n\n  // Try removing \"-free\" suffix\n  if (modelName.endsWith('-free')) {\n    return modelName.replace(/-free$/, '');\n  }\n\n  return modelName;\n};\n\nconsole.log('  Model name normalization:');\nconst testNormalize = ['kimi-k2.5-free', 'moonshot/kimi-k2.5-free', 'grok-code', 'gpt-4o-mini'];\nfor (const modelId of testNormalize) {\n  console.log(`    \"${modelId}\" -> \"${normalizeModelForPricing(modelId)}\"`);\n}\n\nconsole.log('\\n' + '='.repeat(80));\nconsole.log('\\n✅ Test completed\\n');\nconsole.log('🔍 Root causes identified:');\nconsole.log('  1. Model name \"kimi-k2.5-free\" does not exist in models.dev API');\nconsole.log('     -> Need to normalize to \"kimi-k2.5\" for pricing lookup');\nconsole.log('  2. Token usage of 0 may be caused by output format mismatch');\nconsole.log('     -> Need to verify fullOutput contains valid NDJSON lines');\n"

provider prices (Moonshot AI, OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)\n *\n * @param {string} modelId - The model ID used (e.g., 'opencode/grok-code')\n * @param {Object} tokenUsage - Token usage data from parseAgentTokenUsage\n * @returns {Object} Pricing information with:\n *   - provider: Always \"OpenCode Zen\" (actual provider)\n *   - originalProvider: The original model provider for pricing reference\n *   - totalCostUSD: Public pricing estimate based on original provider prices\n *   - opencodeCost: Actual billed cost from OpenCode Zen (free for most models)\n */\nexport const calculateAgentPricing = async (modelId, tokenUsage) => {\n  // Extract the model name from provider/model format\n  // e.g., 'opencode/grok-code' -> 'grok-code'\n  const modelName = modelId.includes('/') ? modelId.split('/').pop() : modelId;\n\n  // Extract provider from model ID to determine original provider for pricing\n  const providerFromModel = modelId.includes('/') ? modelId.split('/')[0] : null;\n\n  // Get original provider name for pricing reference\n  const originalProvider = getOriginalProviderName(providerFromModel);\n\n  try {\n    // Fetch model info from models.dev API\n    const modelInfo = await fetchModelInfo(modelName);\n\n    if (modelInfo && modelInfo.cost) {\n      const cost = modelInfo.cost;\n\n      // Calculate public pricing estimate based on original provider prices\n      // Prices are per 1M tokens, so divide by 1,000,000\n      const inputCost = (tokenUsage.inputTokens * (cost.input || 0)) / 1_000_000;\n      const outputCost = (tokenUsage.outputTokens * (cost.output || 0)) / 1_000_000;\n      const cacheReadCost = (tokenUsage.cacheReadTokens * (cost.cache_read || 0)) / 1_000_000;\n      const cacheWriteCost = (tokenUsage.cacheWriteTokens * (cost.cache_write || 0)) / 1_000_000;\n\n      const totalCost = inputCost + outputCost + cacheReadCost + cacheWriteCost;\n\n      // Determine if this is a free model from OpenCode Zen\n      // Models accessed via OpenCode Zen are free, regardless of original provider pricing\n      const isOpencodeFreeModel = providerFromModel === 'opencode' || modelName.toLowerCase().includes('free') || modelName.toLowerCase().includes('grok') || providerFromModel === 'moonshot' || providerFromModel === 'openai' || providerFromModel === 'anthropic';\n\n original provider for reference in pricing display\n        originalProvider: originalProvider || modelInfo.provider || null,\n        pricing: {\n          inputPerMillion: cost.input || 0,\n          outputPerMillion: cost.output || 0,\n          cacheReadPerMillion: cost.cache_read || 0,\n          cacheWritePerMillion: cost.cache_write || 0,\n        },\n        tokenUsage,\n        breakdown: {\n          input: inputCost,\n          output: outputCost,\n          cacheRead: cacheReadCost,\n          cacheWrite: cacheWriteCost,\n        },\n        // Public pricing estimate based on original provider prices\n        totalCostUSD: totalCost,\n        // Actual cost from OpenCode Zen (free for supported models)\n        opencodeCost: isOpencodeFreeModel ? 0 : totalCost,\n        // Keep for backward compatibility - indicates if model has zero pricing\n        isFreeModel: cost.input === 0 && cost.output === 0,\n        // New flag to indicate if OpenCode Zen provides this model for free\n        isOpencodeFreeModel,\n      };\n    }\n    // Model not found in API, return what we have\n    return {\n      modelId,\n      modelName,\n      provider: 'OpenCode Zen',\n      originalProvider,\n      tokenUsage,\n      totalCostUSD: null,\n      opencodeCost: 0, // OpenCode Zen is free\n      isOpencodeFreeModel: true,\n      error: 'Model not found in models.dev API',\n tokenUsage,\n      totalCostUSD: null,\n      opencodeCost: 0, // OpenCode Zen is free\n      isOpencodeFreeModel: true,\n      error: error.message,\n    };\n  }\n};\n\n// Model mapping to translate     "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env node\n// Agent-related utility functions\n\n// Check if use is already defined (when imported from solve.mjs)\n// If not, fetch it (when running standalone)\nif (typeof globalThis.use === 'undefined') {\n  globalThis.use = (await eval(await (await fetch('https://unpkg.com/use-m/use.js')).text())).use;\n}\n\nconst { $ } = await use('command-stream');\nconst fs = (await use('fs')).promises;\nconst path = (await use('path')).default;\nconst os = (await use('os')).default;\n\n// Import log from general lib\nimport { log } from './lib.mjs';\nimport {
